description: Built-in legacy aggregate targets: aggregate published statistics only (no individual-level data). Each target carries a tolerance used by aggregate-fit diagnostics (implausibility I = |obs - value| / tolerance). Citation research pass 2026-07-11: named-paper identifiers were verified against arXiv/dblp/official pages (see per-target source_status); numeric values were NOT independently re-verified, and the full metadata schema (population, date range, source hash, tolerance rationale) remains incomplete, so every target stays kind=unsupported and comparison gates stay closed.

degree_tail_exponent.source: Scale-free social networks: power-law exponent typically 2-3 (Barabasi & Albert 1999 and successors)
degree_tail_exponent.verification_note: Barabasi & Albert 1999 (Science 286:509) identifier verified via arXiv cond-mat/9910332 on the retrieval date; the abstract confirms scale-free power-law connectivity but states no numeric exponent -- the 2-3 range is a literature convention, not re-verified here.
degree_tail_exponent.source_title: Emergence of Scaling in Random Networks
degree_tail_exponent.tolerance_rationale: scenario_threshold_not_derived: no statistical derivation exists for this tolerance; it is a scenario knob, not a confidence bound
degree_tail_exponent.applicability_limits: aggregate-fit diagnostics only; the numeric value is unverified against any source (null fields above are 'not established', not 'not applicable')
valid_uses: input to the aggregate-fit implausibility diagnostic
synthetic scenario anchoring under stated assumptions

clustering.source: Online social networks: average clustering commonly 0.1-0.3 (e.g., Mislove et al. 2007 measurements)
clustering.verification_note: Mislove et al. 2007 (IMC'07 pp.29-42) verified via dblp on the retrieval date; the 0.1-0.3 clustering range attributed to its measurements was not re-verified against the paper body.
clustering.source_title: Measurement and Analysis of Online Social Networks
clustering.tolerance_rationale: scenario_threshold_not_derived: no statistical derivation exists for this tolerance; it is a scenario knob, not a confidence bound
clustering.applicability_limits: aggregate-fit diagnostics only; the numeric value is unverified against any source (null fields above are 'not established', not 'not applicable')
valid_uses: input to the aggregate-fit implausibility diagnostic
synthetic scenario anchoring under stated assumptions
